0035/00359148.abstract: A rectangular block with a flat top and bottom, and straight edges.
0035/00359148.beginner: The design involves creating a small rectangular block. The block has a length and width that are roughly 0.6 and 0.375 units, respectively, and a height of about 0.075 units. The final shape is a simple, compact rectangular solid.
0035/00359148.expert: Start by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero and a translation vector also set to zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.6, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.6, 0.0) and ends at (0.6, 0.375). The third line starts at (0.6, 0.375) and ends at (0.0, 0.375). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.375) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). After drawing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.6 to the entire sketch. Ensure that the sketch remains aligned with the original coordinate system by rotating it using Euler angles set to zero and translating it using a vector set to zero. To transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch 0.075 units along the normal direction. Do not extrude in the opposite direction of the normal. This operation will create a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular block are a length of 0.5999999999999999 units, a width of 0.3749999999999999 units, and a height of 0.07499999999999998 units.
0035/00359148.description: A rectangular block with a flat top and bottom, and straight edges.

0035/00354394.abstract: A cylindrical object with a rectangular section at the top
0035/00354394.beginner: The design features a thin rectangular block. It has a length of 0.75 units, a width of 0.0597 units, and a height of 0.0019 units. This simple shape serves as a foundational element in the overall structure.
0035/00354394.expert: Start by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero and a translation vector also set to zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0597). The third line starts at (0.75, 0.0597) and ends at (0.0, 0.0597). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.0597) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). After drawing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch. Then, transform the scaled sketch into three-dimensional space by rotating it using Euler angles of 0.0, 0.0, 0.0 and translating it using a translation vector of 0.0, 0.0, 0.0. To create the three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch 0.0019 units along the normal direction. Ensure that the extrusion does not occur in the opposite direction and that a new solid body is created. The final dimensions of the part are a length of 0.75 units, a width of 0.0597 units, and a height of 0.0019 units.
0035/00354394.description: A cylindrical object with a rectangular section at the top

0049/00499945.abstract: The object is a folded paper structure with a triangular base and a rectangular top. It appears to be a folded paper fan or a similar type of paper object.
0049/00499945.beginner: The design features a folded paper-like structure with a triangular base and a rectangular top. The final model stands 0.75 units tall, with a base length of 0.3638 units and a width of 0.4488 units.
0049/00499945.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing the first face, which consists of a triangular loop. Start the first line from the origin at (0.0, 0.0) to (0.3638, 0.0365). Continue with the second line from (0.3638, 0.0365) to (0.0, 0.2974), and complete the triangle with the third line back to the origin. Next, draw the second face, another triangular loop. Start the first line from (0.0, 0.2974) to (0.3141, 0.3829). Draw the second line from (0.3141, 0.3829) to (0.0, 0.4488), and finish with the third line back to (0.0, 0.2974). After completing both faces, apply a scale factor of 0.4488 to the entire two-dimensional sketch. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it with Euler angles set to zero degrees and translating it with a vector set to zero. Finally, extrude the sketch 0.75 units along the normal direction to create a solid body. The final dimensions of the model are a length of 0.3638 units, a width of 0.4488 units, and a height of 0.75 units.
0049/00499945.description: The object is a folded paper structure with a triangular base and a rectangular top. It appears to be a folded paper fan or a similar type of paper object.

0093/00932190.abstract: A rectangular plate with rounded corners and a flat surface. The plate has a uniform thickness and a symmetrical shape.
0093/00932190.beginner: The design involves creating a small rectangular plate. The plate is about three-quarters of an inch long, half an inch wide, and a little over a tenth of an inch thick. It is a simple, flat shape with a uniform thickness throughout.
0093/00932190.expert: Start by creating a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and the translation vector also set to zero. This ensures that the origin of the coordinate system aligns perfectly with the global coordinate system. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. Begin with the first loop, which consists of four lines forming a rectangle. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and extends to (0.75, 0.0). The second line continues from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.375). The third line moves from (0.75, 0.375) to (0.0, 0.375), and the fourth line completes the rectangle by drawing from (0.0, 0.375) back to the origin. After completing the sketch, apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire two-dimensional sketch to adjust its size proportionally. Then, transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional object by rotating it with the Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and translating it with the translation vector set to zero. Finally, extrude the two-dimensional sketch 0.0225 units along the normal direction to create a solid rectangular plate. Ensure that the extrusion does not occur in the opposite direction of the normal. The resulting dimensions of the rectangular plate are 0.7500000000000002 units in length, 0.3750000000000001 units in width, and 0.022500000000000006 units in height.
0093/00932190.description: A rectangular plate with rounded corners and a flat surface. The plate has a uniform thickness and a symmetrical shape.

0019/00194035.abstract: A rectangular block with two cylindrical holes on its top surface. The block has a flat bottom and sharp edges.
0019/00194035.beginner: Design a rectangular block with two circular holes running through it. The block is about 0.75 units long, 0.39 units wide, and 0.34 units tall. The holes are positioned symmetrically within the block.
0019/00194035.expert: Start by setting up a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero and a translation vector also set to zero. This ensures that the coordinate system aligns with the global axes. Next, draw the first face of the rectangular block using a loop of four lines. Begin with the first line from the origin (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0), then the second line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.3936), followed by the third line from (0.75, 0.3936) to (0.0, 0.3936), and finally the fourth line from (0.0, 0.3936) back to the origin. Within this face, add two circles. The first circle has its center at (0.2005, 0.0965) with a radius of 0.0594, and the second circle is centered at (0.5644, 0.0965) with the same radius. After drawing the face, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust the size proportionally. Since the Euler angles and translation vector are already set to zero, no additional transformation is necessary. Extrude the 2D sketch 0.3401 units in the direction of the normal to create a 3D rectangular block. Ensure that the extrusion does not extend in the opposite direction. Finally, perform a boolean operation to create a new solid body from the extruded shape. The dimensions of the final part are 0.7499999999999999 units in length, 0.39356435643564347 units in width, and 0.3400990099009901 units in height.
0019/00194035.description: A rectangular block with two cylindrical holes on its top surface. The block has a flat bottom and sharp edges.
